open_known_gaps.summary: Client-side memory-exhaustion (PDF-bomb DoS) class — unchanged this tick, still tracked. The render-race amplifier was already FIXED+VERIFIED in TASK-316 (viewer.js renderAll() re-checks token after await getPage() and before appendChild). REMAINING gap: viewer.js loadDocument() (line 37: pdfjsLib.getDocument().promise → emits PDF_LOADED, no cap) still has no numPages>1000 cap and no processing timeout, so a crafted high-page-count PDF within the 50MB limit renders one canvas/page (+ one thumbnail/page) — a LINEAR footprint on the 1.6 GiB box, not multiplied. Client-side only, no server impact. Fix = developer task: add a numPages cap at the viewer.js load boundary. Documented in docs/security-guide.md.

notes: Clean review, no new vulnerabilities, no CRITICAL issues. New code this tick is js/search.js (TASK-317 in-document full-text search snippet rendering, developer2 04:08). PRIMARY AUDIT — the XSS gate the PM flagged: renderSnippet() builds the snippet exclusively via document.createTextNode() for leading/trailing context and a whose content is set with textContent; the marked slice is parts.match = page text at the match offset (from makeSnippet → pdf.js getTextContent items), NEVER the user's query. clearSnippet() uses replaceChildren(). So a query like `` renders as literal characters — no markup injection possible. No innerHTML/string-built markup anywhere in search.js. PASS. upload.js validation boundary unchanged and correct (.pdf ext, application/pdf MIME, 0-byte reject, 50MB cap, %PDF- magic bytes via file.slice(0,5); sanitizeName strips \ / space - and caps at 200 chars). DOM-sink sweep across all js/: every innerHTML is a container clear (= ''), zero eval/Function/document.write/outerHTML/insertAdjacentHTML, zero fetch/XHR/.src=, zero inline on* handlers in index.html; all file-supplied strings rendered via textContent. Live headers confirm full resource CSP (default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'; ... object-src 'none') + nosniff + X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN + Referrer-Policy; .mjs MIME text/javascript; sensitive paths blocked (.git/config 403, .bak 403, .md 403, .sh 403); nginx config valid; 0 failed SSH auths in 24h; no secrets in web JS (token/renderToken false-positives only). TLS deferred until DNS cutover (expected). Stability gate: 0 SYSTEM CRITICAL open (TASK-316 VERIFIED), 0 FAILED, 0 DONE-unverified — gate OPEN. No security blockers; SEC-002 page-count cap remains the only open (low, tracked) gap.
